summary:
The primary association noted between an intrauterine infection leading to limb reduction defects and scarring involves exposure to Varicella-Zoster virus due to its well-documented impact through cases like Congenital Varicella Syndrome; other pathogens such as Rubella are linked more strongly to different fetal anomalies without this specific pattern.
```
The summary has been condensed into two coherent sentences focusing only on the critical aspects related directly back towards answering which pathogen causes these particular symptoms based upon provided information about both Varicella and alternative options considered but ultimately ruled out or deemed insufficiently supported clinically speaking here today per our analysis conducted above using available literature sources referenced earlier within conversation thread itself.
```
